hook.text: Both study people. But sociologists survey cities while anthropologists live in villages for a year.

buildup.text: Sociology focuses on modern, large-scale societies. Anthropology historically studied small-scale, non-Western ones.

discovery.text: Anthropologists use immersive fieldwork. Sociologists prefer surveys, statistics, and large data sets.

twist.text: Today the boundary blurs. Anthropologists study corporations; sociologists study tribes. Methods overlap.

climax.text: The real difference is perspective: sociology asks how society works; anthropology asks what makes us human.

quiz.question: What traditionally distinguished anthropology from sociology?
options: Anthropology used immersive fieldwork in small-scale societies
